options: They produce antibodies that coat pathogens and signal mast cells to release histamine
They engulf pathogens by endocytosis, digest them using lysosomal enzymes, and present antigens on their surface to activate the adaptive immune system
They release perforins that punch holes in bacterial cell membranes, causing lysis
They secrete interferon that directly kills viruses in the extracellular space
explanation: Phagocytes (neutrophils, macrophages) engulf and destroy pathogens via phagocytosis; macrophages also present digested antigen fragments on MHC molecules to activate T lymphocytes, linking innate and adaptive immunity.
